serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve carton of packaged less-sugar soy milk in Singapore is approximately 250 ml (250 g), commonly consumed as a breakfast drink or snack beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Soy Milk (Less Sugar, Packaged) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for soy milk (less sugar, packaged), CR ID D02030006. All per-100g values reflect the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat (0.22 g/100 g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14191 (soy milk) as the closest approved analogous source, consistent with total fat of 1.51 g/100 g. Potassium (118 mg/100 g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central soy milk data as HPB record does not individually report potassium.

gi_source.title: Soy milk, full-fat, GI value 34 — International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 34 (low) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for full-fat soy milk, which is the closest approved analogous entry for packaged less-sugar soy milk. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: (34 × 6.76 × 2.5) / 100 = 5.74, rounded to 5.7. The less-sugar formulation is expected to have a similar or marginally lower GI than standard sweetened soy milk given the reduced sugar content.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar present (4.08 g/100 g) in a sweetened plant milk with low dietary fibre (0.44 g/100 g); sodium is low at 9 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should prefer unsweetened (no-added-sugar) packaged soy milk to avoid the vasoconstrictive and inflammatory effects of added sugar; the low sodium content (9 mg/100 g) is favourable, but the added sugar in this 'less sugar' variant still warrants caution.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar (4.08 g/100 g) triggers the sugar-sweetened beverage gate for diabetes management despite the low GI of 34 and moderate protein content (3.12 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should choose unsweetened packaged soy milk (0 g added sugar) rather than this less-sugar variant, as even reduced added sugar in a ready-to-drink beverage can contribute to postprandial glucose elevation; the low GI and protein content are beneficial attributes that are better realised in the unsweetened form.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.22 g/100 g), low sodium (9 mg/100 g), plant-based protein (3.12 g/100 g), and fortified calcium (95 mg/100 g) are cardiovascular-protective; added sugar (4.08 g/100 g) is a minor concern at typical serving sizes
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This packaged less-sugar soy milk is broadly suitable for cardiovascular health due to its low saturated fat, low sodium, and plant-based protein profile; however, individuals with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should monitor total daily added sugar intake and consider switching to the unsweetened variant for optimal benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (4.08 g/100 g) with very low dietary fibre (0.44 g/100 g) limits anti-inflammatory benefit; fortified calcium (95 mg/100 g) supports bone protection
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For rheumatoid arthritis management, choose unsweetened fortified soy milk to benefit from calcium and soy isoflavones without the pro-inflammatory effect of added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soy Milk (Less Sugar, Packaged) is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink plant-based beverage made from soybeans, water, and a small amount of added sugar, sold in sealed cartons or Tetra Pak packaging. It is widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia as a convenient, shelf-stable alternative to dairy milk. The product is labelled 'less sugar' to indicate a reduced but non-zero sugar content compared to standard sweetened soy milk; it contains approximately 4.08 g of sugar per 100 g, of which a portion is added sugar. The beverage has a mild, slightly sweet, beany flavour with a smooth, thin-to-medium body. Nutritionally, it provides 53 kcal per 100 g, 3.12 g of plant-based protein, 1.51 g of fat (predominantly unsaturated), and 6.76 g of carbohydrate. It is fortified with calcium (95 mg/100 g) and contains iron (0.39 mg/100 g), making it a useful dairy-free calcium source. Sodium is very low at 9 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is low (GI 34), reflecting the slow glucose release characteristic of soy-based beverages. Clinically, the added sugar content means this product is not recommended as a first-choice beverage for individuals with type 2 diabetes or hypertension who require strict sugar control; unsweetened soy milk is preferred in those contexts. For cardiovascular health, the low saturated fat and plant protein profile are beneficial. The product is vegan, dairy-free, gluten-free, and halal-suitable, making it broadly accessible across dietary preferences in Singapore's multicultural population.
common_user_queries: Is less sugar soy milk good for diabetics?
Can I drink soy milk if I have high blood pressure?
How many calories in packaged soy milk less sugar?
Is less sugar soy milk vegan?
What is the GI of soy milk?
How much protein in soy milk less sugar?
Is less sugar soy milk suitable for weight loss?
What is the difference between less sugar and no sugar soy milk?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D02030006) with confidence 0.95. GI value sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for the closest approved analogous entry (full-fat soy milk, GI 34). All mandatory fields are complete and supported by approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific packaged less-sugar soy milk product are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D02030006 or in USDA FoodData Central for this exact formulation as of 2026. These fields are set to -1 per the Veritas sentinel-value convention for data not available in any approved source consulted. Saturated fat estimated at 0.22 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14191 (soy milk) as the closest approved analogous source; this is consistent with the total fat of 1.51 g/100 g. Potassium estimated at 118 mg/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central soy milk data; HPB record does not individually report potassium. Cholesterol is confirmed zero (0.0 mg/100 g) as this is a plant-based beverage containing no animal-derived ingredients — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for soy milk. GI of 34 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (full-fat soy milk); the less-sugar formulation is expected to have a similar or marginally lower GI. The input health_suitability entries used condition key 'diabetes' which has been remapped to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas naming rules. Aliases self-check performed: every string value in aliases_by_language has been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
